rationale: det_empty_equal requires structural equality of `r1.receive_state.epmap == r2.receive_state.epmap` (and similarly for send_state). The epmap field is a `HashMap<V>` declared `#[verifier(external_body)]` with `pub uninterp spec fn view(self) -> Map<...>`. The only spec relation available from CSingleDelivery::empty() is on the abstract view (out@ == SingleDelivery::init()). From this I can prove `r1.receive_state.epmap@ =~= r2.receive_state.epmap@` via map_values domain reasoning, but Verus has no axiom that view-equality implies structural equality for the opaque external_body HashMap. Verified with minimal test cases that even `a@ == Map::empty() && b@ == Map::empty()` does NOT prove `a == b` for two arbitrary external_body HashMap values. The synthesized determinism check is therefore not discharge-able from the spec; it correctly identifies that the abstract spec of empty() does not pin down the concrete HashMap representation.
